properties.name: San Vicente Fault
properties.reference: Staller et al., 2016; Canora et al., 2012

properties.name: Apastapeque Fault
properties.reference: Staller et al., 2016; Canora et al., 2012

properties.name: Berlin Fault
properties.reference: Corti et al., 2004; Staller et al., 2016

properties.name: San Miguel Fault
properties.reference: Staller et al., 2016; Corti et al., 2005

properties.name: Caño Negro Fault
properties.reference: Montero et al., 1998, Montero et al. 2017

properties.name: South Coast Fault
properties.reference: Benford et al., 2012; Benford et al., 2015

properties.name: Walton Fault Zone
properties.notes: Slip rates from residual from total relative plate motion - Oriente Fault

properties.name: Cayman Spreading Center
properties.reference: Leroy et al., 2000; Hayman et al., 2011
properties.notes: Likely thin seismogenic thickness, may not produce moderate to large earthquakes.

properties.name: Azuero Fault
properties.notes: GPS predicts 9 ± 2 mm/yr of extension, but this is  not at all evident in the geology.
Topographically well expressed in a rapidly-eroding tropical region.  Looks more normal in E, more sinsitral in W.

properties.name: Rincon Hondo Fault
properties.notes: Interesting expression in DEM. Unclear if it's active but given the inferred kinematics and lack of similar topographic lineaments in the area, it probably is.
Very distinct escarpment in DEM. Unclear of age or activity.
Looks like a transtensional fault, bounding the west side of a graben.

properties.name: Rio Gatun Fault
properties.notes: Rate based on a regional GPS model that may ovrestimate slip rate on individual faults.

properties.name: Rio Gatun Fault
properties.notes: Trace location modified from sources based on DEM

properties.name: Rio Gatun Fault
properties.notes: Geometry modified from source based on SRTM30 data

properties.name: Rio Gatun Fault
properties.notes: Interpreted geometry (the whole fault segment is underwater)

properties.name: Tela Fault
properties.notes: Good seismic profiles in Rogers and Mann 2007

properties.name: Flowers Bay Fault
properties.notes: Cox et al. map southwestern offshore portion as normal, but unclear why

properties.name: Sebaco Fault
properties.notes: Very clear scarp in topopgraphy data and a hanging-wall basin that looks to be actively subsiding (no erosion despite external drainage, river near fault). Nonetheless, no clear quaternary scarps observed.

properties.name: Aeropuerto Fault
properties.notes: Most rapid slip probably sinistral. Hard to locate trace on DEM.

properties.name: Tiscapa Fault
properties.reference: Freundt et al., 2010; Brown et al., 1973
properties.notes: Source of the 1972 Managua Earthquake. Geology indicates mostly normal slip but '72 event was pure left lateral on a very steep plane.

properties.name: Upala Fault
properties.notes: Looks to me like the toe of a big landslide; it's in an otherwise transtensional zone.

properties.name: North Panama Deformed Belt
properties.notes: Understudied structure.  Poorly mapped on low-resolution bathymetry.
